hero_summary: Awj Iraq is a maqam of the Sikah family whose tonic is Sikah (E half-flat). Its defining trait is a descending sayr that begins in the upper register and gradually falls back to the tonic.
short_identity: A Sikah-family maqam built practically from Jins Awj Iraq together with Jins Sikah, defined above all by its descending motion from the upper register.

quick_facts.ghammaz: The upper region in which Jins Awj Iraq appears
quick_facts.character: A descending Sikah branch with a distinctive upper-register launch
quick_facts.common_start: It starts from the upper register, then descends through Nawa, Jaharkah, Sikah, and Dukah before settling on the tonic

sayr.summary: Awj Iraq belongs to the Sikah family and resolves on Sikah (E half-flat). In the practical form reflected by the reference image, it is built from Jins Awj Iraq together with Jins Sikah. It should not be understood as ordinary Sikah, but as a Sikah branch whose identity depends on a distinctive descending path that begins in the upper register and gradually resolves onto the tonic.
resting_tones: Sikah (E half-flat): the final tonic of the maqam
The upper register where Jins Awj Iraq appears: the area that shapes the beginning of the maqam
Nawa (G), Jaharkah (F), Sikah (E half-flat), and Dukah (D): essential passing tones in the descent toward the tonic
motion_notes: Its sayr is clearly descending, and this is not a secondary detail but the main key to understanding the maqam
It begins practically in the upper region, then descends through its essential tones until it settles on Sikah (E half-flat)
If the descending character is absent, the maqam may blur into other Sikah-family branches
If Sikah is not clearly confirmed at the end, the family identity weakens
common_path: Establish the upper register clearly at the opening
Reveal Jins Awj Iraq in that upper region
Descend through Nawa (G), Jaharkah (F), Sikah (E half-flat), and Dukah (D)
Return to Sikah as the final tonic with a convincing cadence

common_modulations: Awj Iraq belongs to the Sikah family, but it is not ordinary Sikah; its identity rests not only on the Sikah tonic but also on Jins Awj Iraq and the descending path
It is close to Huzam by family affiliation, but Huzam is shaped through a different upper coloration, whereas Awj Iraq is defined by beginning above and descending to the tonic
It is close to Iraq by name and modal world, yet in the practical form used here it is clearly presented as a Sikah-tonic maqam built from Jins Awj Iraq together with Jins Sikah
Its auditory profile is not simply that of plain Sikah, but of upper-register tension that gradually stabilizes below
